location.office_time_required: occasional travel for project or team in-person meetings

level_signals: Job title is 'Product Manager L3' but body refers to 'Staff Product Manager'
Minimum of 6 years of experience in product management
Owns cross-functional execution and roadmap planning independently
Compensation range CAD $120,640 - $150,800 consistent with senior/staff IC in Canada

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Minimum of 6 years of experience in product management in SaaS, IaaS or PaaS.
Proven track record managing complex systems: experience managing core workflows and complex state-management systems
Technical Acumen: Ability to partner deeply with Engineering to understand technical requirements, navigate complex system architectures, and resolve cross-team dependencies
Data-Driven Decision Making: Command of analytical thinking skills with the ability to use product analytics and quantitative data to monitor metrics, drive decisions, and measure outcomes

Stakeholder Management & Collaboration: Excellent communication and cross-team collaboration skills, with a demonstrated ability to align and influence Engineering, Design, and compliance stakeholders

Execution & Dealing with Ambiguity: Initiative, ownership, and accountability to break down complex problems, build clear roadmaps, and ruthlessly prioritize competing features in a fast-paced environment
Bachelor's in Computer Science, Engineering, or equivalent practical experience

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience building growth and PLG workflows for products/services related to APIs, developer experience, and onboarding
Experience working with telecommunications, KYC, or other highly regulated ecosystems

scope.surface: Sender Onboarding — sender administration, provisioning, compliance onboarding, and sender recommender experience
scope.business_model: B2B SaaS / CPaaS (developer and enterprise communications platform)
primary_metrics_inferred: Funnel health and conversion through sender onboarding
core_responsibilities: Own and optimize core purchase workflows including sender recommendation experience, exclusive number provisioning, porting and hosting
Drive platform orchestration for unified experience via cross-console ingress/egress, checklist state management, and intent-based routing
Lead cross-functional execution with Engineering, Design, and KYC PMs to embed compliance and autoregistration into customer journey
Drive data-driven decision making using product analytics and dashboards to monitor funnel health and identify friction
Manage end-to-end roadmap planning, prioritization, dependency resolution, and stakeholder alignment

compensation.equity_form: equity grants mentioned as possible additional compensation

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed (CAD $120,640 - $150,800) with bonus target specified (12.5%)
Encourages non-traditional career paths to apply
Clear product surface and ownership area defined
Concrete responsibilities with specific workflows described
red_flags: Role title says 'Product Manager L3' but body refers to 'Staff Product Manager' — level labeling is inconsistent and worth clarifying
Restricted to Ontario, British Columbia, or Alberta, Canada — eliminates candidates outside these provinces
Visa sponsorship and work authorization not addressed in JD
Equity mentioned only as a possibility ('may also be eligible'), not guaranteed
